title: Sigillaria
taxonomy: Sigillaria is classified as a genus of extinct trees within the division Lycopodiophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. This division is also known as the lycopods. Sigillaria belongs to the family Sigillariaceae, which is a group of extinct trees that were characterized by their tall trunks and branchless stems. The Sigillaria genus is thought to have lived during the Carboniferous period, around 320 to 300 million years ago. Sigillaria was a key component of the ancient coal swamp ecosystems, where it played a vital role in the formation of coal deposits.
uses: Sigillaria was a genus of ancient plants that had various uses, but its medicinal properties are less documented.
